name: Apricot Nectar Cheesecake Tart
recipeIngredient: 15 oz Pkg Pillsbury Refrigerated Pie Crusts
recipeInstructions:
Heat oven to 450 degrees. Prepare pie crust according to package directions for one-crust baked shell using a 10-inch tart pan with removable bottom or 9-inch pie pan. Place prepared crust in pan; press into the bottom and up sides of the pan. Trim edges if necessary. Bake for 9 to 11 minutes or until lightly browned. Cool completely.
1. In a small saucepan, sprinkle gelatin over 1 c of the apricot nectar. Cook over low heat, stirring until gelatin dissolves.
2. Refrigerate 30 to 35 minutes until partially thickened.
3. In a small bowl, beat 1 c whipping cream until stiff peaks form.
4. In a large bowl, combine cream cheese, 1/2 c sugar, nutmeg, and vanilla; beat until smooth and creamy.
5. Add lemon juice; blend well. Beat in apricot mixture until well blended.
6. Fold in whipped cream.
Spread over cooled baked crust; refrigerate 2 hours.
7. In a small saucepan, combine 1 T sugar and flour. Gradually stir in remaining 1/2 c apricot nectar. Cook over medium heat until the mixture boils and thickens, stirring constantly.
8. Remove from heat; stir in amaretto. Cool to room temperature.
9. Pour over tart; spread evenly. Refrigerate until topping is set, about 30 minutes.
10. In a small bowl, beat 1/2 c whipping cream and powdered sugar until stiff peaks form. Pipe or spoon around the edge of the tart.
Store in refrigerator.
